capstone-step-4-report-service.starterCode:
from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask

tasks = [BugTask("Fix login", "high"), FeatureTask("Export CSV", "reports")]
for task in tasks:
    print(task.label())

from models.feature_task import FeatureTask

print(summary_report(tasks))

sc-module-11-final-oop-capstone-project-.title: Check the design choice
sc-module-11-final-oop-capstone-project-.prompt: Why does this module 11 final oop capstone require each step to keep all files, not only the file changed in that step?
sc-module-11-final-oop-capstone-project-.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Cumulative project steps must preserve the whole runnable app state.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.b: Because the next step starts from the full previous project state and must still run.
options.c: Because only README files are graded.
options.d: Because imports work even when files are missing.

project-story.title: Module 11 Final OOP Capstone: project story
project-story.bodyMarkdown: This graded project is cumulative. Each step starts from the exact full solution of the previous step, so the project grows like a real multi-file app.
